title: Public theorem release activation ladder
purpose: Represent the explicit release-status ladder separating public-review self-verification evidence from final public theorem emission.

ladder.activationEffect: documents historical sealed theorem-emission artifact without mutating it
declares current public-review boundary and disables theorem emission
records successor report/seal coordinate under public-review language
all current external assumptions are represented as explicit trust-base items
trust-base reduction plan is represented and auditable
repository-level verifier entrypoint exists and emits a boundary-preserving verdict
minimal kernel coordinate exists and is cross-verifiable
NAND semantics, bounded small models, locked-NAND SAT seeds, and complexity ledger are represented
no-hidden-oracle, checker-totality, mutation, coverage, and no-circular-authority seed audits are represented
fresh clone, container, multi-platform, determinism, and regeneration seed audits are represented
status transitions are explicit and machine-checked by this ledger
would represent unrestricted final soundness rather than bounded seed evidence
would permit internal activation candidate only after unrestricted final soundness is represented
would permit public theorem emission only after all blockers clear

transitionRules: The project does not jump from accepted package evidence to public theorem emission.
Each activation status must be represented as an explicit ordered ladder entry.
No blocked status may be treated as complete while its blocker appears in remainingBlockers.
PublicTheoremEmissionCandidate may be complete only when publicTheoremEmissionAllowed is true, finalTheoremReady is true, and activeFinalNodeIds is non-empty.
The current ledger must keep publicTheoremEmissionAllowed false, finalTheoremReady false, and activeFinalNodeIds empty.
nonClaims: This release ladder does not activate public theorem emission.
This release ladder does not clear Release.UnrestrictedFinalSoundness or ExternalReview.Acceptance.
This release ladder records ordered status transitions; it does not by itself discharge unrestricted final soundness.
This release ladder treats external review as a reproducibility and audit layer, not as a substitute for repository-internal evidence.
